There's an enormous difference in both size and power!
I can't emphasize this enough—! Commission's half off! Isn't this act of courage worth a few dozen gold coins?!
Hahh... haahh...
...Not bad, kid... A faint light envelops your blade. Is this your last gasp?
Ha, excellent! I'll rip your arms out, and gag you with your own flesh—
I must win... I must...
What am I doing... Are you meant to kill your opponent in the knight sports?
I don't need my shield... I can't block his axe...
He's hurt too, but he doesn't care... He's like a monster, and he enjoys killing...
How should I... face an enemy like him... An enemy? Isn't he a knight?
A knight...?
'To be a knight is to be the noble light that illuminates the land...'
Ahh—!
O-Owww...
...Came around finally?
Auntie...? What... happened...? Oh.
Do you remember now?
...Did I lose?
Yep.
But Ingra passed out too. Neither of your scores changed at all.
I-I see. Phew... That's good—
It's not good! You're hurt!
You should've forfeited the match, going up against an opponent like that! It's just a single match! There are so many ways for us to get those points back!
If you just want to get a title and keep your family's nobility, there's no reason to go that far—
I... I never thought about forfeiting, not even once.
Maria!
Do you have any idea how lucky you are to get out of a fight against Ingra in one piece?
If you get hurt again, what good does getting into the Major do to you?!
...He's really strong, but...
He's not like the other knights. He comes from an insanely well-off family, and he's only in the competition to hurt people. He lives for it. That's the kind of man Olmer Ingra is.
Maria, if you get hurt like this again, I won't let you fight anymore.
I—
Listen to me!
F-Fine...
Don't move so much. Get some rest.
That match ended in a draw, but you'll have more and more companies trying to woo you... Marcin and I will turn them down for now. Try to rest up and get better.
Yeah...
Oh... What's that sword?
...That's the sword I used to carry. I dug it out while you were training the other day.
But why are you carrying that sword specifically...?
(Even though Zofia's already...)
No reason at all.
But if something bad happens to you because of him, I'll kill him personally, even if it means I'll have to face the National Council.
Y-You're way overthinking it...
...Can I see it? It kinda looks like it hasn't been used in a while.
No. Get some rest.
I might start to rust if I lie in bed all day...
No dice.
Please, Zofia! Auntie! I won't ask for anything else! I'll be good and rest up!
*Sigh*... You're becoming more and more like your sister...
Take it. Don't you do anything weird with it. I'm quite fond of it.
Eh... This is pretty dusty...
That's the proof that I treasure it!
Where are you two going, carrying all that stuff with you?
We're going to pay Maria a visit.
I will never forgive Ingra for doing that to our girl...
Spare her the noise and give her some peace and quiet. Zofia will take care of Maria.
Well, I can't just sit around and do nothing! Did you see that last swing Ingra took at her? What happens if that comes back to haunt her?
We've got an old knight and an old craftsman here. There's bound to be something we can do to help.
What if you run into Młynar instead? Do you think he won't throw you two out the door?
...That prick wouldn't dare do that to us when his old man was still around, and I know when to run if it comes to it!
...Hmm, the moment the two of them left, I got a new customer. They just might be the albatross around my neck.
Welcome. It's been a while since we've had such a beauty in here. What can I get you?
Hm...
...This one has a pretty name. Let's go with this one, the 'Thorn Tear.'
3:49 P.M. \ Clear \ Kazimierz Kawalerielki Alliance, a private open-air loft
...I see you've arrived, Mr. Mob.
Wait. It's you. I saw you back at the arena and you said—
No. That's neither here nor there. Now, don't be so wound up. Take a seat.
Allow me to introduce myself again—please, call me Czarny. I'm one of the rotational managers, currently responsible for a division of the Knights Association.
I'm also an executive of the Mieszko Group Major & Publicity Department, and have had the great fortune to be selected as Divisional Spokesman to the General Chamber of Commerce.
Th—The G.C.C.? Pardon me for my incredible rudeness earlier, Mr. Czarny...
Forego the formality. I'm no critic of your... vernacular, 'Greatmouth Mob.' Or, perhaps, I should be calling you by your real name?
N-No need...
Haha, I only jest... Now, why don't we take a look outside. Glorious view, isn't it?
There are some old types that hate this reinforced concrete jungle, the city of seamless day and night. But if you ask me, it is what symbolizes our strides towards civilization.

Our vast forests, grassland expanses, are a priceless part of Kazimierz, true, but our hardy Kazimierz hands built up great skyscrapers where the forests parted. It's something we ought to be proud of.
It is the only way we find shelter when a Catastrophe strikes, the only way form a front that can stand firm against our foreign enemies. Kazimierz is now prosperous, a rapidly developing place...
Would you agree? Mr. Mob?
Course! You're right on the mark!
But Kazimierz's strides are far from over. The road we have to walk is still long.
For example... Mr. Mob, take your hometown, our 'City of Saplings and Artisans,' Ognisko.
In the thousand miles around it, how many deprived villages lie? How many troubled lands unafforded the protection of a city?
Y-You really know your stuff, for sure. I was outta there, like, twenty-plus years ago. Can I, uh, ask why you're telling me all this, though? Truth is, you're kinda bugging me out here...
Your frankness is another part of what draws me to you. I've come to make you an offer, Mr. Mob. One that will take us in joint strides. You've been poached.
...Me? But I just took a down from the Roar Guards Company...
Please, relax. This contract is confidential, sealed by none other than Mr. Pavel himself. Now, from today on, your employer will be...
S-So I'm a Mieszko guy now?
You could put it that way... but let me nitpick it very slightly.
Yeah?
From now on, you're MY guy, Mr. Anchorman.
Hiyaaaaaah—!
...Let's stop here for today.
Huh? But I can still...
Overdoing it will undo you! You only just finished recovering. We're all sturdy Kuranta, but no-one torments her body like do!
Alright...
—Could you help me brainstorm about those Arts I mentioned, then, auntie?
...Didn't we already go over this yesterday?
I watched this tape of Leithanian Knights battling last night, and I think I could pull from some parts of it to—
Last night?
Ah...
So you were up late again! I've said before, you need to get your rest!
Looks like someone's back in good shape. Them two old cranks can rest easy now.
Oh! Marcin? Is something up?
Something? Eh... yeah, you might call this something.
This is, um?
Sponsors who came knocking at your door. Thirteen, in all. And there's some pretty high ranked corps looking for you...
Whoaahh. Glorious Shield's the weapons manufacturer, right... so even companies like these are into keeping competition knights?
Oh, they're all Knightclub invitations. They put the numbers there to make you gasp. They're looking to make you a fine puppet, that's all.
Maria! You wouldn't let these capitalists lead you by the nose, would you?! Remember your sister here can outbid these and then some, anyway!
Wait... I'm not falling for anything! Just, auntie, you spending money on me like that makes me feel kind of bad...
Money, my high-heeled foot. If I want to spend it, I'll spend it. I didn't buy this land by agonizing about it, I'll have you know.
(Is that why the 'gardens' here are just 'fields' now...)
Hah hah hah... fair to you, I know you turned every last one of them down. Made a fine family out of nothing but your prize money and a few years of retirement. Hats off.
That said, I'm only here to hand these papers over. Saves Młynar the hassle. Rest of it's up to you two alone. Think it over and if you're okay, come relax over at the bar sometime.
Well... what do you say? Maria?
I've said before, but I don't want our family crest to become a corporate logo... and I know I can't stand toe-to-toe with the rest of them, either. Not as I am right now.
...You're going to press on?
Mm-hm. This isn't just about the money.
When I think of 'Nearl,' the name my grandpa and my parents protected all their lives, I can't imagine it was all just for the numbers and lines of the stock exchange.
So...
Maria... Alright, it sounds like I'll be throwing all of these away, then.
If we're going to make the cut for the Major, we need to get a lot more points, fast—that's the biggest hurdle we face, as independent knights without a Knightclub to hide in.
Remember Szewczyk? Poor man, taken for a ride by his own sponsor. And yet he built up the points to rival nearly an entire team, just on sprint races, and duels alone.
Meanwhile, you had the Knightclubs all wrangling the rules for point transfers, arranging it so their trump cards didn't even need to fight the preselections...
So I'll have to get past all of those Knightclub teams, soon enough...
That's right. That's why we're going to have to take some special measures... if we're not wasting a single qualifier, we only have so many options.
Such as—
Welcommme, one and allllll, to the Fireblade Arena—!
Fifteen teams! Fifteen knights!

Today's match is a one-and-done! We'll see plundering! Crazy battles! Tight escapes and desperate chases! Welcome to the third largest arena in Kazimierz, manmade to unleash the deathmatch within every knight!
What's that? You say one match only earns you one point!? Where does THAT happen!?
Not here, not now! In this match, any valid attack against a fellow knight means a valid score, and that means points! Points, for fat stacks of cash!
But who cares if a bunch of point-grabbers play a game!? No, I know! I know what it is you all really wanna see—

You wanna watch the heroes fall! You wanna watch the great stars crash to the ground! Thaaaat's right! This season's melee matches have a new rule in the mix, the 'Points Off' clause! You hear that, knights? Risk and reward in one!
Where's the spice when your points never drop?! In this match, we're following the 'Predator System!' Let's keep it simple, sister: when someone goes up one, someone else is goin' down one!
Oh, yeah! Believe it, I'm scrapping to see what the do-it-alls do! Our rising stars, swallowed up! It's unprecedented, folks! A match to flip the rankings head to toe!
Thought you could qualify for the Major by wining it up in a bubble bath?! You'd better risk it all right here, right now! Get ready to rumble, because the world's watching!
Don't forget, our sponsored drones from Raythean Industries will be broadcasting every knight's viewpoint non-stop! Our eight registered Knights Association referees won't miss a single detail!
Now, the knights are making their final checks, the crew are fitting their armor with the sensory chips! How many stars will be born today, and how many are gonna eat dirt and taste crushing defeat?!
We can only wait—for the knights—to enterrrrrr—!
What the hell? This guy again? Isn't he supposed to keep to Roar Guards territory?
Guess he found his own rising star, now.
Nah... 'Points Off?' They ever have a 'Points Off' in the melees before?
And they announced this all-new system right before the match started. What, they can just do that?!
Didn't bet on the Association being all this capricious, did we... and it doesn't bode well.
Looks like all fifteen knights are readied at their places! Once the crew are out of the way, the match will officially begin!
A full sixty minutes of battle! Unless every knight is down for the count, this battle goes to the very last second!
Even if your points for this season take a sub-zero dive, you'll still be in the game! Wanna see backs against walls? Last-gasp attacks? You're in the right place, folks!
Hey, Maria Nearl.
—Who're you?
Mmm... just one of your fans.
Alright—! All fifteen knights are signalling ready!
Let's make it quick. The moment the Brassrust Knight healed up, Ashley—my Ashley—sent him straight back to the hospital. Man, the Ingras have been down on their luck for weeks now.
I declare—
Now, Ashley took me right to the playoffs, so I've been a little panicked. Thought I'd try and make up a few points here. But I didn't think I'd find you too, young Nearl.
—Why don't we go for a little tête-à-tête?
A what?
—Let the Fireblade Melee—begiiiiiiiiinnnnn—!
And eight remain—
No, remain? Flip that around, almost half the Knights in the field have already exited due to injuries!
Wait—as I was saying that, it looks like 'Hoarhair' has taken a point off 'Springwater'—oh! And 'Drifter' Dorka barges in to pick them off! Now that's what I call productive!
Everyone pulling moves on everyone else—this is the glamor of the Fireblade Melee, folks! 24-carat violence!
Light, heed me—!
That's all you're capable of? How the hell did you beat Ingra?!
Your swordhand... is nothing compared to my auntie's!
Heh, I'll tear you right off that scoreboard, 'Nearl!' Watch this!
Ah—behind you!
Uagh—?!
Huh?
Fancy seeing you again, young Nearl. You've got nowhere to run now.
And right after 'Gałązka' comes 'Flametail'! Whoa, way-way-way, how many times has someone come for the Nascent Nearl now? You gotta get your own show, Maria!
God, I hate that guy. You're too loud!
Anyway, we're running out of foes to fight. Looks like we finally get to size each other up, right?
—If all we're doing is measuring, I'll gladly keep you company.
Ooh. Not bad. Your eyes got a lot better since you messed up Plastic.
Hup—
One blink. Open. Shut.
(Below?!)
Gugh—?!
Ohh? Dodged that, did you?
(What did she do just now...? She wasn't that fast—more like just too dexterous...)
Ahh. I see how you held up for so long against Ingra and Szewczyk now... you've got a better foundation than I thought.
Whislash taught you all of that, huh?
Nn-huh. You've got a sharp eye too. You know, you learn pretty quick.
But—
And now you're down one point. I think me and this format are gonna get along just fine.
Keep on like this, and every point you got from capping Plastic's gonna go down the drain, you know that?
Hnnfh—!
Urrrrraaahhh! You stinking little squirrel—!
Can't you just lie down and bite it for one second?!
Hah! Knocked away your weapon! What are you gonna do now, huh?!
Just pick them back up?! Dumbass!
Don't waste your breath! You'll meet your end right here!
—Watch out!
What the hell? Arts—no, you can't be, you're not even armed!
No, you... you... you can't be—
Good night.
Jeez, Louise...! What a good day to earn some free points... Alright, where were we, little Nearl?
(Here she comes again—!)
D'ja know? Fun fact, I'm Infected.
Huh? Infecte—
And another point. It is SO easy to distract you.
...But when you said Infected, you weren't just saying it, right?
You used your Arts directly just now...
Isn't it a shame?
You mean?

When the Blood Knight took the title, they went 'yeah, we'll officially let Infected be knights'—but when the Radiant Knight got infected, they chased her completely out of Kazimierz. Doesn't that suck?
Margaret...
Your debut battle was at the Roar Arena, right? All you needed to do was take your paperwork to the Knights Association, sign for your tournament credentials, and then head backstage to gear up...
Do you know how much—how MUCH—an Infected has to do to get here?

They think it's funny to lock you in a cage of gloompincer first. They think it's funny to make you trample the blood of other Infected, leaving one footprint at a time... they make you win back your humanity, to prove you're a normal, living, breathing person.
Welcome to the bottom line. They weren't gonna give the Infected any respect for a single second. 'Just throw them into the machine. Make them one more symbol of our progress.'
...Don't you think I have the right to hate it?
Our sights are set on every noble knight who thinks her opinion means the world... we'll go under their own rules, cut them right where it hurts.
Honestly, I'm not here to knock you out of the game... after all, I respect the Radiant Knight from the bottom of my heart. Being Infected doesn't sway me there.

Let's call the points I've already taken a fee, and here's a lesson on behalf of 'Whislash.' From the match where you beat Ingra, I can tell you still don't understand what the actual significance of the Major is.
Blind enthusiasm? Riding your own efforts, tuning all the warning signs out? In any ideal scenario, worst is you'd fall flat on your face. But this is the Major. That'll kill you.
Keep it up. Whatever.
...Wait.
Huh?
Light? Same thing as when you beat Ingra...
—Hiyaah!
Are you kidding?!
That's one point back, Flametail!
Goddamn. Didn't think you were so stupid thirsty for it—
—But hey, now you look like the Radiant Knight's sister!
We step back, put some distance between us. Our blades face off again.
...This feels strange.
We just talked a little, simple as that. Facing her, I can't feel any of the same immense, nauseating pressure Szewczyk and Ingra had... is it just because of her nature?
Yet she's so strong. Ngh. And fast. And masterful.
You're smiling? You're that confident?
...Am I?
Eh. Give it a try and you'll find out.
Alright, then...
Aaaaaaaand—! Tiiiiiiiime!
We stop in our tracks as soon as we hear the announcer.
By just a hair—by just the slightest hair, we could've been touching the other's armor.
Lightly, like she's just messing with me, 'Flametail' taps against my shield.
Maybe you'd have taken one more point off me here. Maybe not. Who can say, Maria?
Could've been two, maybe.
Hahahaha—you know, from the stands, it's hard to tell just how fiercely stubborn you are, Nearlie.
And our refs are finished judging! At the end of this free-for-all, only four remained standing!

Yo, audience! Here's the deciding moment your wallets have been waiting for! Even if your favorite knight started out getting knocked around six ways from Sunday, don't you lose the light! The Arena's here to welcome you back any time!
Fourth place! Earning ten points, the arena was his to walk, and he avoided almost any direct battle! Our survivor with smarts, 'Gałązka' Daniel!

Third place! Hunted by the whole arena since the word go, and without a moment to breathe, she might be our biggest winner on the points exchange tonight! Our dainty, charming independent knight, Maria Nearl!
Second place! In the starting skirmishes, he beat three knights with his own hands! He made the arena's corner his own with his tremendous shields! The walking fortress, 'Limestone' Marko!
And our champion tonight, with twenty-two fresh points to her name! A near uninterrupted combo of victories! The super-rookie who never drags her feet—'Flametail' Sona!

And that win of hers means a mean mountain of money, so let's make some mega noise—! Coming up! We'll be announcing the lottery draws for each prize pool! Tonight, while the knights strike the sky, the audience strikes it big! All at the Fireblade Arena!
Ah, welcome, dear Miss.
Having the fortune to meet the youngest ever to join the Kazimierz Armorless Union, the mighty Platinum, I admit that I, humble Czarny, am profoundly terrified.
Uh, can we not with the niceness, Spokesman, sir. You're the ones who yelled for me anyway, right?
Hardly, hardly. I'm merely the General Chamber's mouthpiece, working as its directors will it. How could I dare think to command the mighty Platinum?
You say that, but, like, two of them are just busy and then the three above make everyone else do all the work forever, right?
Ugh, this sucks... I hate overtime. I already had enough work before you called me... Seriously, I wanna go on vacation to Sami once the Major's done.
As long as the General Chamber agrees, I can arrange you a Sami countryside villa right this moment.
I gotta wait until work's over, thoughhh—
—What do you want the Armorless Union to do?
We have many problems on our hands, dear Miss. Infected out of line, curious rumors, knights drawing a little too much attention...
But I trust that at the nod of the mighty Platinum of the Armorless Union, none of these will be a problem at all.
I'm back! Where's the chef? Let's get him going!
He's got a big meal to make tonight, and Maria'll be here soon... she needs a feast and then some! Don't say it was me who called for it.
Madam Zofia, you have a guest. One who claims urgent business with you.
A guest? Now?
They can wait until Maria's back—
Ah... apologies, Zofia, for coming uninvited...
You're the one who... *sigh*... I remember you had your own little company, and then you broke into my place like this, thinking you'd make off with all my sponsorship rights.
Well, out with it. What now?
I, uh, there's some... I have some info here... probably some of it's rumors...
But don't tell anyone! True or false, the event plans here are all top, top secret...
But I think you've guessed that already... just treat this as me paying you back... and, uh, I hope things turn out well for you...
I haven't seen a good knight in a long time... who had a happy ending.
And this is your glass.
Thank you. Thank you.
The Roar Guards Company offers a wealth of beverages to its members. I truly envy them. They possess the luxury of standing here and observing each knight's magnificent display.
Tell me. You are a good person, correct?
Er...
Have you broken the law before?
Have you ever committed a wrongdoing?
No, of course not, never...
So in other words, you are a diligent worker who earns his pay for himself and his family. Doesn't that make you a good person?
Y-Yes, if you put it that way, I'd definitely be a good person, a good person like so many others in Kazimierz...
Indeed, so many others. Here. Let us toast to the good people.
Ah. Cheers.
Tell me. If there were some who sought to deprive good people of their work, to deprive good people of their way of life, could those possibly be good people, now?
What do you...?
Even the countryside farmers, done tending their fields for the day, will look forward to that competition knight broadcast from the city.
For hundreds of thousands... possibly millions of Kazimierz's industrial people, the Major is a part of life. And they are all good people, by and large, my friend.
So, you mean...
Suppose, one day, the Major was gone... that overnight, the business had vanished.
Who would now support our campaign knights' expenditures, which grow annum by annum? Who would vie for Kazimierz's economic standing, and so stave off foreign incursions?
My friend, tell me. Are those who would see that happen—bad people?
You are a good person, and they would deprive you of your own living to crown their ancient sense of 'honor.'
We all rely on others to pave the way. We've given the vast majority—even the Infected—the chance to live...
And so those 'bad people' would ignore that, their country and all its citizens, for their own inconsequential, self-centered consolation.
My friend. You are a good person, correct?
This place is Nearl's regular bar, right? Whoo-wee, now this is what I call Kazimierzey!
Hey, Boss! Think Maria Nearl might come here today?
That's down to your own square luck.
Business been good lately?
Thanks to Maria.
When's the last time we got so many in here? Back with Zofia?
Knight's the magic word. Most people're here just for that. Don't even ask me which of the media monkeys said it...
Not happy even for the money?
Wasn't the idea to give the two of them somewhere nice and quiet to relax?
Hey, Marcin. Think about carving it into this seat here, 'Kowal ONLY.' Don't wanna wait for a spot next time I come by.
Cough up a naming fee and I'll agree.
Tch.
Whoa, whoa, Nearl's really here! It's literally her!
Quick! Don't let the chance slide, go get a picture!
Er, thanks, thanks... picture? You mean me? Sorry, I'm a little...
Alright, alright, make way, you lot—
Hold on... you're Zofia! The 'Whislash' Zofia!
Hey, the rumors were true! 'Whislash' Zofia is the actual coach of the new Nearl!
Miss Zofia! I've watched your matches before! They really shook me!
(Oh, well, we're done for.)
(A-Auntie! Think of something, quick!)
Maria, I love you!
What did you just say?!
Hey, take your fights outside.
You've gotta be at least this old to brawl in here.
—Mister Kowal! Mister Vogelweide!
All customers, listen up! To celebrate our two old regulars and their outstanding performance, prices today are all 20% off!
Everybody sit down and get your orders ready! All drinks and snacks are limited supplies only! Once we're out, we're out!
Discounts? Could it get any better?
Hold on... hold on... the bald guy there, you think he's...
Just old Marcin, nothing more. Go on. What's your business?
Marcin...? You're the real 'Tremoriron' Marcin... *sobbing*... Today's just too blessed... Give me a 'Marcin Special'...
F... finally... I've never seen this place so lively before...
Bless your heart, Marcin. I'll help you recoup the costs later.
Hah hah hah. If you think 20% off'll sink my bar, you'd be selling old Marcin short.
...Crafty hand, you.
W-What? Can I keep drinking or not?!
Place's empty, old boy. Who're you even drinking with?
I—*geurgh*—
Don't throw up here—do it outsi—God! Your old damned bones!
Let's get down to business, then. Closing hours and all.
Closing hours are where we're booked in, hm?
Hah hah hah, they're when this little bar's really open for service.
Maria... you've gotten enough points already.
You've been winning fight after fight. It's incredible.
Th... there's been an element of luck to it...
As long as you can maintain the score, dig your heels in on it until the end of the season, that'll be the best plan... But I'm worried about something else.
Auntie?
...Maria, the next match is a race. Races don't make for great changes in points, so it should be a safe bet.
And then after that, don't participate in any more matches.
Wha?
B... but, just staying...
You've done plenty enough. Victory's at hand now.
Surely you've noticed it too... Maria, a lot of people are paying attention to you right now.
We just need to keep the pace. Wait until the points are finalized, and you'll win a knight's title, and we'll have achieved our goal.
Okay? Just listen to your sister. This will all be for the best—
But this... this isn't going to change anything at all... If I don't keep going, the Nearl family's still going to be stuck in this position...
Uncle Młynar wouldn't approve of doing things this way, either...
Młynar? He's like talking to a brick wall! He wouldn't approve no matter what you decided to do. You know that better than anyone, don't you?
Just don't think about it too much, okay? Maria... this is all for the best. It's this or nothing.
Auntie... what happened?
You don't know how deep the Major really goes... You don't know what it is you'll be up against at all...
Auntie!
...In the arena, victory will never belong to a knight. Victory will only ever belong to Kazimierz, belong to... to the corporations.
If you truly don't want to work with them, you shouldn't have joined the Major in the first place. If we keep doing things your way... Maria, we need to know where to stop.
Zofia. What did you find out?
...That Słoma employee I know told me about some rumblings. Maria's popularity has thrown her into the Knights Association's executive sights far sooner than anyone else...
If Maria doesn't stop where she should, they'll start making moves! No matter what it is they're planning for us, we don't have to grin and bear any of it!
So, Maria, listen to your sister, okay?
I... I don't get it... If we don't want to be controlled by them, we have to bow to them? Where's the difference?
Maria! This is no joking matter—!
I know—
You could die in the arena!
...'Flametail' said so too. I haven't seen what the significance of the Major really is...
I know I might genuinely have no idea what cruelties she was referring to... but I didn't enter the tournament for fun, either!
I don't care about injuries, I don't care about accidents, I don't even care if I have to sacrifice—
That's enough!
Ah... I'm really sorry, Auntie, I didn't mean...
That's not a 'sacrifice' at all. And I don't want to watch you suffer for any of this, either...
I see how you're thinking. You really need to learn when enough is enough!
...I don't think I should stop here.
Even if I don't pass the qualifiers, even if I fall short of my goal, I should be fighting for my own cause... I should be striving to win until I can't win anymore!
...Alright, I get it.
Auntie...!
I'm convinced you should stop. You don't understand the Major. You can't understand... the sheer outrage Margaret's actions caused back then.

If Kazimierz really was a fair country, everything you've said could be true. But that's not how it goes. Any idiot can see how flimsy all our rules are, and how trivial it is to leave them in pieces.
You're still an untitled, unaffiliated, independent knight, Maria. I can apply to the Knights Association for a suspension on your tournament campaign.
So there.
...Auntie!
Cool down, Maria. And let Zofia cool down a little too.
She means you well.
...I know.
But Zofia's right. A knight doesn't win the Major by will alone.
The corps were only interested in giving 'the new Nearl knight' the chance to take root and start growing. When they decide to reap you, they'll come out with their sickles, no second thoughts.
Knights get one right, and it's to apply to the events... beyond the Major, corps have every rank and result in vice grips. Knights don't get a choice any way or how.
If Zofia's heard the truth, and the company insiders are making noise...
Maybe we should be picking up on the signals. Paying attention, at least.
Weugh... whassappenin'? Swear I saw Zofia storming out just now...
What's the long face for, girl? Didn'tcha win? What's to argue after winning?
You two, stop making it worse...
...I-I'm going home.
I have a race coming up, and I still need to do my equipment maintenance...
...*Sigh*.
...*Sigh*.
(She's beautiful, but she carries the scent of blood... is she a knight?)
(Maria Nearl. Young... even younger than she looked in the material.)
(Shame.)
What exactly is a knight competition?
Mr. Szewczyk, what do you think?
...Forget the song and dance, Czarny, I'm busy.
Out with it. What are you up to?

I'm very sorry to say... the Roar Knightclub has decided to switch you out of pole position in their starting lineup for the Major. Their new safeguard knight ranks highly just as you do, and his Arts should be useful to the team.
This... is nothing like what we agreed...
Of course, your down payment will still clear. But the rates for a knightclub's reserve are an invariably different question. You understand this, correct?
Now, now... don't make any hasty moves. Your child is celebrating a tenth birthday soon, I hear. You needn't lose your job now over assaulting someone as insignificant as myself...
Roar Knightclub will still be paying you plenty. You needn't make any efforts for at least the rest of the year.
I need the lineup corrected, for God's sakes! I...
That'll be up to your own performance, Szewczyk. There's always a way to live on, is there not?
...You're not wrong.
You could've just sent out any old envoy to tell me this. Why'd you need to meet me personally? Are all spokespeople this bored for something to do?
Ah... don't misunderstand me. I didn't come out here specifically for you. I was only here to make sure of some event arrangements.
It seems we've been a little lax in parts. Our divisions have each encountered very minor bumps in the road, which some offenders are seeing fit to take advantage of.
...Are you threatening me? Threatening 'Plastic' Knight Szewczyk to take on your dirty work?
Nothing of the sort.
Then what are you trying to do here?
Right now, I have two particular knights who are currently working to enter the arena... but, unfortunately, they clearly do not have the points to do so.
Pfah... If you want a knight to make it in, you have your ways...
Don't put it like that. There are times one must respect the rules, and keep some conduct undisclosed—
—Making use of the law versus violating it is a night and day affair, after all. The fundamental difference lies in whether the parties involved possess the capacity to do so.

Just give it one nod of assent, and we'll add an event for the transfer of points, along with all its formalities. Of course, in much the same manner, I can settle of some of your complications with the Roar Knightclub...
How would you feel about that?
—OUT! The Strider Knight's sole-mount accelerator devices are non-functional! That sudden brake knocks him off right before the finish—!
ACROSS THE LINE—! The winner, from Gloryfall Knightclub, is the Wisteria Knight! And right behind, Maria Nearl—hounding that medal!
With his accelerators out, the Strider Knight has fallen all the way from second place to seventh—! It's not a good day to be the Strider!
It just goes to show how crucial a knight's equipment is!

And Ellen Select Technologies, who provide intimate maintenance, repair and upgrades to your equipment with their comprehensive member plans, have provided a no-expense-spared package for our knights!
Phew... runner-up, huh?
(This way... I'm another step closer to the Major...)
(Auntie Zofia...)
Oh, there's an unexpected customer.
Please, sit wherever you'd like.
Marcin... it's really you? That tremendous knight of days gone by runs this little bar now?
They stripped old Marcin of his knighthood long ago. Now all he does is eat up his savings.
How'd 'Plastic' Szewczyk find his way to this little place? Tell you the truth, this bar doesn't see all that many active knights in it.
If you're tremendous past, Maria's tremendous present. They carried a story about your bar on the entertainment news last night.
Hmph... tremendous all around. I'll have a 'Red Edelweiss.'
This one's on me.
My pleasure.
Roar Guards' training camp's a long way away. I won't let you waste the trip.
So, you've come to enemy headquarters to spy on us, then? Right that old newspaper-worthy wrong for the Majors?
...I'm not going to be in the Majors.
Huh... Roar Guards' decision? Thought you and your sponsors were on pretty good personal terms?
Ha. Personal terms. Managing a relationship with a corp is like dating a Catastrophe.
As always, the decision came from the next floor up...
Knights Association?
Worse than that... try the General Chamber of Commerce.
Hum.
I know what you want to say, Marcin. Up there, you're thinking, 'how's a tiny little Plastic Knight get into such a big mess?'
I still know my limits, and nobody goes this overkill on a knight who knows how to follow plans, so their problem is elsewhere.
A rumor's spreading door-to-door. People saying the Radiant Knight was never even Infected, that her exile from Kazimierz was more complicated than that...
Like hell I know.
Put your guard down... I'm not probing you here, Marcin.
I just want Czarny to learn there's a price to pay for pissing about with nobles... and I'm assuming he's got one eye on Maria Nearl too, doesn't he?
...The knights are joining hands and protesting the Association? This sort of thing won't ever end well.
I'm just trying not to be ridden roughshod.
Thanks for the drink... Marcin, one last word... when I was young, it was watching your matches that made me finally decide to become a knight.
Went the wrong way back then, looks like.
No. I'm ready to go my own way now, aren't I?
See you next time, old Marcin.
Szewczyk, my own last word... stay safe.
...Hmph. No knight ever needed to bow to the peddlers. Roar Knightclub'll see things my way.
Hey, you've been tailing us for ages now. Time you showed your face, right?
Oh, the overbearing type... No, I do NOT like fans like you...
'Wait—that bow—are you—?!'
—Thought I'd say that and keel over, Armorless Union assassin?
Hmm... so this is how you're molded. Way you dodge about everywhere, no-one's actually seen your looks before. Real gloomy line of work you're in...
Don't get the chance everyday—
...Hey.
—to have a real scrap, you and us.
Ah—auntie—
Oh... uncle...
I look away from you for a second, and you somehow dig yourself even deeper.
And when are you planning to stop?

The leadership has been asking me time and time again about you. Every day I work in the Publicity Department is spent sorting out the trouble you cause... Half of my hours at work are lost to waiting for direction from the leadership.
Go to the Knights Association this moment and exit the tournament. You don't have the faintest idea what you're doing.
Is that what you wanted to say...
You will not kill yourself over this, Maria.
You are a child of the Nearl family. You ought to know restraint.
These annoyances all lie low in the face of the Major, so this is up to you to do, but you've already earned yourself plenty of attention.
Abandon the idea that you can force your way into the Major. Abandon, also, any idea that you've yet faced the strongest competition knights.
But I don't want to just sit and watch while the Nearl family's nobility gets stripped—
—Vainglory.
*Sigh*...
I've made it clear before that the Nearls do not need the approval of the Knights Association, obscene as it now is.
Have you somehow come to believe that without their recognition, our ancestors' deeds would cease to exist? Nonsense! For all these years, the Nearl family stood alone to defend our name!
What one cannot do, one must abandon. When one does not know how to give up... that blind alley is their end.
Uncle...
Margaret has influenced you too much, Maria. She could never recognize her own ignorance, and you are not to blindly follow her example.
Don't give me that look. Have I said anything wrong? And surmounting the rulesetters within their own rules? Lunatic ravings.
You know Margaret's temper. What she ultimately despised was the commercialization of the competition knights. I thought she had some backbone...
And what happened? Back against the wall, she still chose the path of competition, to flaunt her 'convictions.' Yes, indeed. 'Convictions.'
And could she change anything?
Uncle... don't talk about Margaret like this...
She could not.
The one thing she changed was how the corporations treated the Nearl family! The one thing she accomplished was forcing my father, bedridden as he was, to clean up her mess!
'The Radiant Knight' is a title she should've never been conferred! She has no power to shoulder any glory of the sort!
Uncle!
So what about you...?
Aren't you... working for the corporations, even now?
You forbid me from competing while you go play servant boy to the corporations and watch the Nearl family's knighthood slip away? You can stomach that?!
You're still young. For that, I won't blame your ignorance... but you should show respect to your elders.
This again...
The skyscrapers... that the people of Kazimierz built, generation upon generation, cast the shadows that hide you in your hometown, shielded by your elders. What could you see from this safety?
Did you truly believe you had something? As nothing more than a young, fledgling knight?
When the sun dawned over the frontier fortresses, the Ursus skyscrapers drew their line in the clouds on the horizon.
The tower attendants recreated the motions of battle in the arena to please their nobles. The young steam knights donned the armor of old to temper and hone their skills.

Bounty hunters and robbers razed one destitute village after another, while the great cities were absorbed in hopes of building ever more arenas. One could call the harvest of a village versus the tax it pays comical.
But all of that paled to the last sigh my father uttered on his deathbed. Not for Margaret, not for your parents—it was his lament given to all of Kazimierz.
How could you not disappoint me?
You're right, Maria, you're right. I am good for nothing, nothing more than a corporate, captive burdenbeast.
So. What about you?
...Director? No, just a quick personal matter to tend to... No, of course work takes priority over personal...
The project meeting? Today? No, I didn't forget... Apologies, my greatest apologies, I'm very sorry, you're right...
I'll be back there momentarily... What? No, he doesn't understand the scope of the project... Yes, I'm sorry...
Then if... If he could present in my stead... Yes, my apologies... The manager's responsibility? I... Yes, this will be the last time. No, there won't be a next time.
You may terminate me if this happens again. Yes, of course, I promise.
I am truly deeply sorry, Director.
...I do not have much time to waste, Maria. Give me an answer, now.
You won't change my mind, Uncle.
You're just like Margaret... you disgust me.
Your parents would find no pride in you. Take some time to calm down. Start thinking hard about who you really are.
...Please, just stop talking!
Ignorant of the world and its untold ways... Pray for blessings. That is all you have left.
You tell them to drink, but what about you? How many pints have you downed?
Pipe down, old bones. I just gotta fire up the engine in me—
—What's wrong, girl?
Not looking too good there. You and Zofia haven't patched things up yet?
I'm fine... Has Auntie been here again?
She was pacing around right by the door, but then she left just a minute ago. She's worried about you.
I say you go find her.
They've already decided my next opponent.
If I can win, do you think my auntie... do you think everyone will understand me then?
There, girl...
...Things aren't that simple, Maria.
Hey! Watch it, old boy, what're you blabbering?
The reason Zofia doesn't want you in the Major's not just because she reckons you'll lose or get hurt...
The competition isn't fair. See, the moment you set foot up higher, you're up against more than just your opponents...
Think corps. Corps playing other corps. Families fighting other families.
Not even them Adeptus watchdogs'll stick their hands in. Between the Knights Association, Grand Knight and all the directors, they make a three-way power tussle messier than you might ever imagine.
Course, I wouldn't tell you to give up, Maria.
'Think it through, look at all the difficulties you'll face, and only then should you press on. ' When you figure that one out, go knock 'em all the hell down for us old-timers to see.
We've got a new face—
—You're not welcome here. Kindly leave.
Don't be so cold, my old friend, dear Tremoriron Knight.
Your sacrifice brought a chronic heat to us all. I'm greatly respectful of your disposition within the arena.
Who the hell're you?
...The level-2 knight, Vogelweide... the former second-order knight. Or ought I be calling you Batbayar? I'm not sure if I'm pronouncing it correctly...
Hey—
Ooh, no, no, let's not bring the daggers out. I'm certainly no knight, merely an assiduous civil servant...
Cool it! Vogelweide!
...If I had my bow on hand, you wouldn't dare talk to me like that.
I appear to have made a transgression. If I offended you, I deeply, profoundly apologize.
Leave that little two-faced act by the door. What are you doing here?
...I'm a mere working stiff. As is natural, I'm here to relay to Miss Maria Nearl some arrangements the Knights Association have made.
Miss Maria?
Ah... um, here...
Your next opponent has been duly decided.
No reason to come in person for this.

True, but I do prefer to handle things personally. It is the only way I ensure I have an exact grasp on all manner of matters that may arise in the workflow... Of course, there's a selfish element as well.
I can hardly contain my curiosity regarding the sister of the Radiant Knight Margaret Nearl. The young knight who's all the rage.
There are many seeded players, hopefuls for the Major, who have hardly the popularity to match up to you, Miss Maria. It is a strength you bear as a competition knight, an advantage.
I hope to see you make appropriate use of this advantage. Of course, I also possess many... high-grade business contracts.
When compared to any of those sponsors who sought you out in private, I can guarantee that these are all corporate and Knightclub affiliations beyond your imagination...
We're long sick of your sales pitch. Maria here takes things her own way.
Hear, hear! You've taken all the glory out of knighthood. It's profane!
Glory? Ah... right, glory.
The campaign knights garrisoned the frontier fortresses at dawn, and competitors of every standing brought value regardless to Kazimierz—
Glory, where is glory? Has it vanished?
No.
No-one can excise glory from Kazimierz. Not even if the hundreds of thousands of Kazimierz companies, big and small, were to join forces as one.
So have knights been reduced to tools of war, eyes blinded by profit? Have knights abandoned glory themselves, forgotten their pasts?

No and no. To say that is to look down on the knights. No director would dare entertain the lie that knights have become marionettes. For what reason would the flourishing of the competition knights bring us sorrow?
*Spit*! Who are you to talk about glory?
Kazimierz can't even remember what the past—
—Past glory, the spirit of knighthood, ah—the great transcendental soul! The sun that beamed in history's hollows!
Do I have that right? But neither spectator nor tourist need spirit, and it's hardly necessary for us to make a show of it for them.
Slippery little... I could tear your face off right now.
Ah, let's not be so hasty. Glory, yes, what a wonderful thing, what an impeccable thing, and still it exists upon every knight family's crest—
—But, most unfortunately, the Kazimierz people no longer need it.
It has not been abandoned, nor drowned beneath. It has always been here. The simple fact is people nowadays... do not need it.

I can hardly even say 'abandoned' is fair, my friend. When you buy an all-new intercity net-connected digital television, and you stow away your old radio into the cupboard, do you call that 'abandoned?'

Rather, it's forgotten. Not the kind that carries a spiteful meaning. It is the pure kind of forgetting, the one that results from the progress that scientific advancement and new modes of life bring.
How can you denounce progress itself? My friend?
Don't call me 'friend' this or 'friend' that. There's not a single friend of yours here.
Finish up fast and trot on home. Maria isn't on display here.
No, my friend, you are wrong once again.
Of course I do not stand in front of a knight simply to demean her status. No, but every knight has the obligation to recognize her own responsibility to Kazimierz.
Knights are no exhibits, but the most noble and lavish of the showcases, for all the world to gaze upon the beauty of Kazimierz! I dearly hope you can all understand this.
Ashley... you think we went overboard this time? Hey, Ashley, come on. Did you pass out?
Ggh, it's so loud... shit hurts...
Haha, yeah, I get you... but we have to keep up the pace. Want me to carry you?
...In hell!
They haven't caught up with us yet... What street are we on, even?
There's... their breath, from the front! Turn left!
Okay—hold on tight—!
...When's the next match?
Oh, my apologies, I'd almost forgotten what I was here for.
It's three days from now, and your opponent will be the Left-hand Knight... 'Left-hand' Tytus Topola.
As a sidenote, Miss Maria, if you accept any of the Knightclub proposals I've put forward to you, then even supposing you have no luck in the Major—
—I can still guarantee the legitimacy of your noble knighthood will remain secure.
I'm sure Miss 'Whislash' Zofia, and the Radiant Knight, far away wherever she is, will both be reassured.
...I appreciate your kindness.
I'll make Kazimierz recognize the Nearl family. I won't let all my ancestors' efforts go to waste. I WILL prove that the Nearl family deserves its knighthood!
Well, well, you'll prove... in the competition... Tell me, will you prove?

Is cooperation with a corporation not the greatest proof a knight could have of her status? When you face your most ardent fans, tourists from all corners of Terra, the loyal clientele of Kazimierz, what is it that you so desperately want to tell them—
—What will you prove?
...I will prove...
—Pardon?
I'm sorry, please wait a moment.
Hello? Speaking. What? No, I'm currently... Right, I understand. Please, don't worry.
Regretfully, Miss Maria, it seems I cannot wait for your answer.
And to you, gentlemen, pardon the offense I've caused, and I hope you have a pleasant evening.
Keh. Get outta here.
Well, then. Let me bid you farewell. If you ever change your mind, contact me at any time.
Makes me want to hurl seeing that corp slime face.
Say, V... Why'd he know your...
...I'd like to know myself. Since my grandpappy passed on, been the better half of a century since I heard a soul call my old name.
And his pronunciation was a pile of shit.
Things are getting risky. Knights Association and the corps are looking into us now. Looking into all of us.
Someone might've already started making moves.
Don't look like that, Maria. You're not alone here. We owe a hell of a lot to your old Grandpa Nearl. We're all—
Anyone here—!? Sorry, we got a casualty—
...The Flametail Knight?
Nearl...? Uh, let's make this quick—could you help us out here a bit?
Ugh... well, this sucks... I was looking forward to our arena matchup. Now I get to remember you as my nurse first, huh?
B-Both of your injuries...! Come on, just come with me, quickly—
Wait, what are you all—
Maria, you look after the wounded. Don't need to worry about anything else.
Kowal!
Here's your bow, you old bones!
Looks like they brought company.
...They didn't come with.
Pfah. Who're we dealing with? Who sics themselves on competition knights in broad daylight?
That kid's an Infected. Let's go see what's what. Might need more than just Maria for this.
—Marcin?
...On my way.
The previous Platinum harbored a secret love for his target, and so for the sake of protecting the object of his affections, he died at the Armorless Union's hands.
As a result, the girl of today was pushed hastily into the position, our 'youngest Platinum.'

...She is a gifted mark, make no joke, and I must say her strong potential makes her unignorably suited for the role... but in all other respects, she is far and away one of the least mature to ever assume the position.
I-I see... Why are you telling me this? The Armorless Union's existence should be classified... so aren't you...
Oh, do lay your concerns aside. It's simply been on my mind quite a bit as of late. Had I not made the effort to get to where I am today, I would hardly enjoy the privilege to ponder it.
Did you know? Our present Platinum was elected to the position by the very Platinum before her, doomed to die as he was.

A great deal of food for thought... Not to mention why the Armorless Union would listen to a traitor, to say nothing of how electing this girl would have absolutely no impact on his own death sentence. For what reason did he do this?
Before the arrow of his own colleague shot through his chest, did he think to cry out? To whom? Of what? To what end? Would it have been that easy?
If I remember correctly, you a Słoma employee, correct?
Y-Yeah... so far in the event, I've been responsible for liaising between the major companies and the Knights Association...
Errand work takes quite the toll.
It's no problem. If I can contribute to the business, that's what I'm here for—
No no no, your work is what they pay your salary for. You needn't use such humble language.
You apparently had your own business before. What made you abandon it?
Ah... I didn't have what it took...
What distinguishes failure and success may be more than ability. Perhaps you have chosen the wrong path to walk, or perhaps you walk as I do now.
P-People like me only...
In Kazimierz, anything is possible.
Zofia... feels like an age since I saw you.
Oh, Marcin...
...Have you seen Maria?
Something cropped up. Had her head back home.
I see—
—I smell blood. Hold on. What are these bandages?
Has Maria been hurt? Who was she fighting? What happened?!
I told her not to be rash! Why doesn't this silly girl—
Steady, now. It's some other kid who got hurt.
...What happened?
Someone went for the Ashlock and Flametail Knights out of nowhere... Ashlock got injured, and by a mighty stroke of something, they ended up in here.
This morning, they said they didn't want to cause Nearl any more trouble and left.
...Do things like that just happen?
They're in a hell of a tight spot, but our hands are tied.
Seen the news this morning?
What's Maria doing now?
Competing.
Competing in what? Against who?
Unguarded one-on-one, just like with Ingra. Head to head.
Against 'Left-hand' Tytus Topola.
Hold on—who?
Blade Helmet Knightclub commander. Noble knight, big commercial name, been as far as the Final 16. Tytus Topola.
But... But there should be no way Maria gets matched against him at her point level, and his social standing is...
Someone came looking for Maria. It wasn't good.
She's just like Margaret used to be. Obsessed with charging forward, no idea how to deal with the corporates.
You think... she's like Margaret?
Margaret KNEW what she was doing. Maria only got like this after falling for her own wishful thinking about her own ideals.
She's not even clear what's in front of her, and she doesn't know what to do about it. She can't keep pushing upwards like this!
Maria was so much more carefree before this... She doesn't need to be the kind of knight you and I were, does she?
You might be right, but you need to remember she's got her own mind for things. You can't decide it all for her.
She's still real young, and uneasy at that. Maybe her being carefree was all just so she wouldn't worry you.

——There is no one that you need to protect!
I will become your shield, so——
Move aside, I will blast them both to death!
——I'll do my best!
Guh... She abandoned her shield! But your Arts!
...This, impossible... That warhammer is her staff, she...
Are you ready, Maria?
Yes!
To be a knight...
Is to be the noble light that illuminates the land!
The Radiant Knight who charged onto the field has abandoned her shield after just a few short exchanges! Hold on! Wait!
Remember the two-handed warhammer craze that followed the Radiant Knight?! Watch her swing that hammer, her shield long forgotten! This is the old school Radiant Knight!
She's out there, against all odds! The return of the legend! The National Council could drag her away right after this match. This could be our only chance to witness the glory of the Radiant Knight!
This... these Arts, this glow...
Why are you allowed to give off that kind of light so impassively...? You arrogant little——Do you really think yourself the sun?! Radiant Knight!
How long has it been since we saw Margaret put her shield down?
Her light... It's hot.
She will be fine, right?
I've never seen her fail, after looking like that.
The girl is a lot like Nearl.
Yes, that's her sister.
Hehe... What a cute little knight. I can see the resemblance.
...Arrows... ran out!
Gaa——! Your Arts!
He's forming arrows using his Arts... I'll block them!
Leave the rest to me.
Can't penetrate——this damned light!
...The audience is quiet.
Margaret doesn't have an advantage. Those two Sarkaz are strong... and work well together.
But I don't feel like Margaret will lose... I don't even think she is disadvantaged...
Her techniques are mesmerizing... Her demeanor is so calm, yet her light burns so brightly...
It reminds me of Old Nearl, when he was young.
Un-unbelievable! Totally unbelievable!
The radiance from every swing of Margaret's warhammer completely dispels her opponent's Arts! Even the cracks the Radiant Knight steps on are filled with light!
What amazingly brilliant Arts! Combat techniques of this level!
This is definitely befitting of a champion! The true strength of a knight——!
Haa... My wounds aren't hurting as much anymore... Is this Margaret's Arts too?
(Caring about me even during a battle like this——)
Gaa——! My hammer...!
No weapon, then, with my hands, I'll tear you apart——!
Kill her!
Margaret has a strange expression on her face. It's...pity?
For these two Sarkaz? Because they are called knights? Or is it... because of their circumstances?
...Maria.
Ah, I'm here!
I'll leave the support to you.
——Yes!
Do it!
Yes!
Repent.
At this moment, the entire arena descends into silence.
No one has realized that the match has ended. There aren't any knights boasting about their victory, nor are there the defiant howls of the losers.
The corrupted Sarkaz kneel on the ground——They have not fallen over, and the Radiant Knight does not strike.
She looks down upon the two Sarkaz, then takes a knee and extends her hand.
There is no reply from the Sarkaz.
You should not give up on your destiny.
No...
As if unwilling to bow to the two young girls, they remain silent, unable to stand.
The Radiant Knight turns.
Maria.
Eh? What...?
We've won.
This is unbelievable! Can you believe your eyes?!
Because I sure can't! The Radiant Knight who suddenly entered the fight! The Knights Association that let it happen! Oh my god, I, this——
I've never seen a situation like this in all my years of commentating! Though I don't know how the National Council will react, it doesn't change the fact that they won!
(Mr. Mob.)
(...Oh, you're here!)
I am deeply honored to announce to everyone!
(Mr. Spokesman, what should I do?)
(Acknowledge her.)
The winner is! The young and miraculous Maria! Aaaand, she who has returned from her long exile, the pegasus that everyone knows, the Radiant Knight! Margareeeeeeeeet Neaaaaaaaaarl!
(That's right, Mr. Mob. I really do appreciate your ability to fan the fervor of the audience.)
(Hold the fort for a while, someone will come to handle this.)
Cheer for her——!
Zofia?! Where are you going?
Marcin! Where are they——Ugh, that, that one?
Is it really Margaret... Why'd Margaret come back now?
N-no matter what, she's an exile. Why didn't she tell us——
Alright, both of you calm down. Młynar might have known about this... But for now, we need to leave.
The reporters and the crowd are going to swarm this place. We have to find a way——to get the Radiant Knight home.
Radiant Knight! That's the Radiant Knight from last season!
Make way, make way! Let me get a picture!
Everyone! Everybody! Please remain calm! No matter what happened, we have to make sure that the competition adheres to the proper procedures all the way up to the end!
(What should I do next?! You can't contact the spokesman?! Idiot! Go look for him at the VIP seats——!)
Hold on! That, that's the Whislash Knight! The Whislash Knight suddenly rushed towards the Radiant Knight from the crowd——
Zof——
Eh——A-Aunt Zofia?!
You... Why are you back?!
——D-did she just slap the Radiant Knight? Huh? Eh? This looks like it'll be in the papers tomorrow——
I'm sorry. But I've returned.
...That slap was for Maria.
I'll spare you my own.
Thanks for sparing me... But, you used your right hand just now... When did that happen?
...Now isn't the time to talk about that. The National Council will definitely not allow an exile to return to Kazimierz.
We have to leave before the reporters and the National Council show up.
R-right now? But the competition results haven't...
It doesn't matter! She's here and that's way more important than the competition!
Nearl.
Careful, Liz!
...You don't need to worry so much. Alright, hold my hand tight.
These two Sarkaz...
Don't worry, they're my friends. Shining, can you do me a favor?
Yes.
I... I fainted... These are healing Arts?
Who... you?!
Sarkaz, you... you! It can't be, you? Healed me?
Your injuries are very serious, but they are not from this match. The scars of drug usage may never be removed. You should take better care of yourselves.
Go away——Don't touch me! Guh...
Wait... You...you are...
——Nearl.
Yeah, I know.
Those two assassins from the Armorless Union are still watching us... Now is not the time to celebrate.
A-Armorless Union? You——
Time to run? Nearl?
——No.
A victorious knight has no reason to scurry away like a coward. If they wish to stop us, just let them try.
Nearl, over there.
Ah... It's Mr. Kowal and Mr. Vogelweide. Have they found something?
What do you think?! Of course they're preparing a way for you to escape!
The three of us will help keep the crowds away from you, but the National Council will definitely be paying you a visit. You'll have to deal with them yourself——
——Margaret!
Yeah?
It's really great... that you came back.
The usual place, don't be late.
Of course.
That's... Nearl's family?
She's come back home. We can all see her joy, no?
So, let's give her some space. She's the Radiant Knight after all. No one in Kazimierz can touch her.
Right.
We'll regroup with Amiya later.
Margaret raises her head.
She used to hate this land with every fiber of her being.
...Maria.
Ah, yes?
...Let's go home.
But this is her home.
...You're here. What about your little follower?
...He's the next one.
Ah... How cruel.
Just tell me the answer.
Well, aren't you straightforward...? You already know the answer, why ask me?
Maybe you can live better.
No... Maybe I will die on the way to my exile. Maybe you will be the one to do it.
...Maybe. Don't blame me, if I am.
Of course.
It won't happen through the National Council. You'll have to leave by tonight. Is there anything you need to take care of?
Szewczyk has a young son. He was present during the incident.
Right.
I wish to ask you...
...To make sure that he never has a chance to speak the truth.
It's me. The spokesman incident is over——
......Platinum.
...Who are you? I'm looking for the director...
Doesn't matter. You should have received the authorization code for this terminal.
Since you know about this channel, you aren't one of the two Lazurites. So...
Just keep your suspicions to yourself and refrain from looking too hard.
What I am saying next will be between the 'Armorless Union', and nothing to do with the 'directors'.
......Alright.
A squad of campaign knights crossed into the Twilight Wood in secret. This went unnoticed by the General Chamber of Commerce, so it likely has something to do with the Radiant Knight.
Silverlance...?
The Lazurite will take full responsibility for this matter, don't trouble yourself.
Haah? Since when are they so responsible?
Your mission is to monitor the Radiant Knight in their stead.
The Radiant Knight... that's a pain.
——So they've got an even bigger pain over there?
Still unclear. The ripples from a single stone spread far. The return of the Radiant Knight has brought us a lot of trouble... In fact, we're devoting too much of our attention on her.
...One more thing.
No matter what, do not take action against the Sarkaz with the Radiant Knight.
Those two Sarkaz... You seem to know a lot about them.
The Confessarius and the Radiant Knight also belong to another 'company.' Will this matter be so simple...? I'm not sure.
Just handle your own tasks properly and don't mess up like before.
We'll be busier.
Ahh... It's the phone again... Are they looking for Mr. Czarny?
But Mr. Czarny... has been gone for a while.
...Hello?
The spokesman?
Ah, no. Sorry, Mr. Czarny is currently——
Wh-what?
I dialed the spokesman's number, and you answered.
Therefore, you are the spokesman.
Huh? Wh-what? I don't understand——
Name.
Name.
Err, please call me Malkiewicz. I used to work for Słoma Food Company, until Mr. Czarny invited me to work with him...
S-Sir? Is Mr. Czarny——?
Good day, Mr. Malkiewicz.
Ah, sorry, Miss. I thought that——
I am the Director and CEO of Słoma Food Company, you should be familiar with my voice. Mr. Malkiewicz, you are now officially dismissed.
Huh?
From now on, you no longer belong to the Słoma Food Company, nor the Mieszko Group——Please keep in mind at all times that you are representing the General Chamber of Commerce.
When the Major ends, you may choose to stay or leave. You will be generously compensated based on your performance.
Right now, there are three tasks waiting for you. You have no right to question them.
Firstly, ensure the smooth continuation of the Major. This is of the utmost importance.
Secondly, guide the public opinion regarding the Radiant Knight and related matters. We are not asking for 'damage control', what we want are 'benefits' on top of that.

Thirdly, some contacts from the Armorless Union will be arriving at your location within three minutes. Supervise them as they carry out their duties. If there are any issues, report directly to the General Chamber of Commerce's Board of Directors.
Wait...! I don't want to be...!
Is there anything you wish to say? Spokesman Malkiewicz?
I-I don't think...
No, I... I understand...
Very good. I wish you the best of luck.
Haa... A dream... This is definitely a dream...
I heard the good news. Czarny's authority has been completely transferred to you, Spokesman... Malkiewicz, right?
What do we do next? You might need a minute. I don't mind taking a break.
I-I don't understand. Why me...?
Wait, wait. Aren't you the same, Platinum? Why did you allow yourself to be pushed into this position? What should I do...?
Who knows? Maybe they just wanted to see someone become as nasty as they are.
Don't think too much when you're in Kazimierz.
But... Just look at me... I can't even afford a decent suit. I...
Right, Mr. Czarny must have arranged some tasks for you. You should know what the Armorless Union is doing——
No idea.
I only know what I'm doing.
Th-then, what are you doing?
Oh... Usually people stop asking when I answer like that. You...
Never mind. I screwed something up a while ago and now I have to deal with it.
Five Knight-Assassins were made while hunting a target. Then they disappeared.
The Armorless Union w-went missing?
...Are you really prepared to know the truth?
I... I have no other choice.
Really.
Pay close attention to the 'Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub' then. They're more than just competition knights. They openly... oppose the Armorless Union.
...The Radiant Knight has returned?
Word on the street is she descended from the skies and rescued Maria.
Rescued?

The Knights Association... No, it probably came from higher up. They arranged that match for Maria. The audience all believed that it was just a thrilling performance, but those with discerning eyes could tell...
They were going at it for real. If the Radiant Knight was any later, Maria would be dead.
...Are they giving up the game already?
Maybe we gave them more credit than they deserved. These things happen in every season of the Major——They don't care.
What they care about doesn't matter. I never expected them to understand, but to think they don't even want to keep up appearances...
What's important is that in Kazimierz, no one cares.
They're telling people we're out of competition for a while because of a training accident...
...That's ugly.
'That's ugly.'
...Pfft. Hahahaha. Hey, what the hell's up with that? Isn't that totally good for us?
If they had gone through the National Council instead of scurrying around in the dark, we'd really be in trouble.
You... You let yourself go after just relaxing for a bit.
Mhmm... Our opponents couldn't wait any longer and acted first, but ended up giving us the win instead. How could I not be happy?
When those two finish their jobs, we'll finally get to the main point.
What? Didn't you get your fill in the arena?
Tch...... The more I cross weapons with that trash, the more I hate those knight nobles. What disgusts me the most is that I used to be one of them.
*Sigh* So complicated.
They don't have a penny's worth of dignity or faith. The knight nobles are total sellouts now.
Don't get yourself worked up. This topic always does that to you. Don't worry, they'll get what's coming to them.
Though, before that, we should straighten out the enemy right in front of us.
——The Armorless Union.
We'll expose them out in the open for all to see, yeah?
Hey Margaret, how long has it been since you've been home?
Your room is still as it used to be! I clean it all the time... Oh, I think we sold the furniture though...
B-but, it's fine! At least——
Maria, just seeing you all makes me feel like I've returned home.
...Margaret.
Maria, your equipment...
Oh! I went and borrowed your old stuff... Hehe, how is it? Fits me nicely, right?
Yeah, it's a nice fit. You've really grown up.
Hehe... I'm as tall as you now.
You've worked hard, truly. I wasn't able to talk with you properly in the arena earlier...... I've really missed you, Maria.
Ehh... Sis, you're making me embarrassed...
You've fought alone until now, but you survived, you've persisted.
I was aware what kind of impact my decision back then would have on Uncle and you, but I never thought that you would——
Margaret!
There's something you absolutely have to do, that you'd never back down from, right?
...Yeah.
Then you didn't do anything wrong!
Besides... I was never able to become like you, tough inside and out...
But I've started to understand. At least, I can think about what Aunt Zofia has always said... I can think about what a knight truly is.
Even though I still couldn't become like you... I've been able to get where I am today, with everyone's help!
If there were ever any beliefs that I held on to, they came from you! Because I too am a member of the Nearl family!
B-But, Margaret, you can't ever leave again, not like how you left six years ago...
I'll follow you this time, no matter what happens to us in the end... I, I believe in you.
Maria, you... Heh, you've really grown up.
...E-Ehh? Where did all of that come from...?
Anyway, just go upstairs and have a look——
...Margaret.
Uncle... this is...
Keep quiet.
Margaret. What did you come back for?
Don't you know what it means for you to appear here?
You rescued Maria... But why did you return to Kazimierz?
Do you know how much your father and grandfather sacrificed to 'send you out'?
Yet you dare to appear here so brazenly... Do you think that the Association and the corporations aren't watching your every step?
...I will join the Major.
Impossible.
I will retake the glory that belongs to the knights.
Glory is meaningless.
Meaning is in the eye of the beholder.
Kazimierz has changed. They have changed.
—Yet I'm still here. We're all still here.
Don't be greedy... Margaret.
I imagined you would calm down after leaving Kazimierz... But this is your answer?
You're wasting my time.
Uncle...
Enough.
In the time I've spent talking to you, a mountain of documents has piled up waiting for me to handle. Don't interrupt my work...
Leave Kazimierz before things turn worse. You've made a life for yourself elsewhere, just don't come back anymore.
Uncle! How can you say that?!
Maria.
Margaret...
Uncle Młynar, I understand your decision. I still respect you just as I did back then.
I also hope for your support.
—Understand?
You know nothing of what I've seen, what I've done. Yet you still hold on to your idealistic thoughts...
I was once lucky enough to catch a glimpse of hope. I have also experienced the shadow of war.
I'm merely choosing to continue believing in my faith, and to fight for it.
...Kazimierz will not change one bit, no matter how much the Radiant Knight sacrifices, no matter how much she wins.
Of course. Of that, I'm very clear.
Even so...
Even so.
...Draw your blade, Margaret.
U-Uncle?!
If you insist on being so stubborn, I have no choice but to to teach you how to 'give up' the hard way. To fall by my blade is far better than falling into the shadow of the Armorless Union.
...As you wish.
Those stupid competition knights are just a bunch of trash willing to be manipulated for the sake of money.

If the young mistress of the Nearl family were to have an accident as well, don't even bother calling this campaign knight a knight anymore! I'll go flip the National Council's goddamned table myself!
——That's what the old man said.
...Senomy, there's no need to mimic him so convincingly...
Ehh, but he specifically instructed me to convey the exact message.
You don't need to listen to everything he says.
He even said that if the Grand Knight didn't agree, he'd go greet the Radiant Knight himself, even if he had to hobble all the way there.
He also said that if you sighed and cupped your forehead, reluctant to answer, he would——
Alright, alright... I know how unhappy he is. He hasn't changed at all, still causing a ruckus in the Court Sprawiedliwi Kazimierz every day over what few accomplishments he has...
Don't forget he is still an offender.

But the National Council, totally corrupted by the General Chamber of Commerce, has no right to convict my old man. They don't representing the citizens or the law at all. They're just representing themselves and the merchants behind them.
...He taught you that as well?
No, I figured that one out myself.
*Sigh*...
Is that all?
Ah, there's one more thing.
...Yeah.
The people from Rhodes Island seem to have arrived.
